pullRequest.title: PLAT-9854: Update repo-standards-orb and use the new catalog v2 commands

nodes.body:
_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

<details>
<summary>🧩 Analysis chain</summary>

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
#!/bin/bash
rg -n -C2 \
  'repo-standards/create_catalog_v2\b|repo-standards/create_catalog\b|create_scale_test_deployment_and_persist_id|create-scale-deployment-with-tempest|scale-tests-workflow' \
  .circleci/static-build.yml
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 1561

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Find the repo-standards orb definition to see if create_catalog is still defined at version 1.2.0
find . -name "*.yml" -o -name "*.yaml" | xargs grep -l "repo-standards" | head -20
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 90

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Check for comments or notes around the scale-tests workflow and create_scale_test_deployment_and_persist_id
# that might explain why it wasn't migrated
rg -B5 -A5 "create_scale_test_deployment_and_persist_id|scale-tests-workflow" .circleci/static-build.yml
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 1271

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Check git history or comments in the PR context about planned migration of scale-tests
git log --oneline --all -20 2>/dev/null | head -20 || echo "Git history unavailable"
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 1577

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Look for any deprecation notices or migration guidance in the codebase
rg -i "deprecated|migrate|scale.*test|catalog.*v2|create_catalog" .circleci/static-build.yml | head -30
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 1176

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Check the orb version specification in the current file
rg -n "cerebrotech/repo-standards" .circleci/static-build.yml
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 117

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Look for any .git/config or orb imports that might show where the orb comes from
cat .circleci/config.yml 2>/dev/null || cat .circleci/*.yml | grep -A5 "orbs:" | head -30
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 3269

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Search for any changelog, version file, or deprecation notices in the repo
find . -type f \( -name "CHANGELOG*" -o -name "DEPRECAT*" -o -name "*.md" \) | xargs grep -l "create_catalog\|repo-standards" 2>/dev/null | head -10
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 44

---

🏁 Script executed:

```shell
# Check if there's documentation about the migration in BUILD or README files
find . -type f \( -name "BUILD.md" -o -name "README*" -o -name "MIGRATION*" \) -exec grep -l "catalog\|orb" {} \; 2>/dev/null | head -5
```

Repository: cerebrotech/domino

Length of output: 102

---



</details>

**Finish migrating the scale-tests deployment to catalog v2.**

The `create_scale_test_deployment_and_persist_id` job still calls `repo-standards/create_catalog` (line 110), but this orb bump targets v2 commands. The `scale-tests-workflow` reaches that job through `create-scale-deployment-with-tempest` (lines 1143–1146, 985), so this incomplete migration may cause the workflow to fail if `cerebrotech/repo-standards@1.2.0` no longer exports the legacy command. Migrate `create_scale_test_deployment_and_persist_id` to `create_catalog_v2` to match the v2 migration of all other callers.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

In @.circleci/static-build.yml around lines 908 - 917, The job
create_scale_test_deployment_and_persist_id is still invoking the legacy
repo-standards/create_catalog; update it to use repo-standards/create_catalog_v2
to match the orb v2 commands used elsewhere. Locate the
create_scale_test_deployment_and_persist_id job and replace the create_catalog
step with create_catalog_v2, ensuring you pass the same inputs (e.g., filename,
base_branch, debug or any results_* keys) and adjust parameter names if the v2
command uses different names so the deployment ID persists the same way.
```

</details>

<!-- fingerprinting:phantom:medusa:grasshopper -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated reply by CodeRabbit -->

✅ Confirmed as addressed by @urianchang

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment by CodeRabbit -->
@coderabbitai This is not a concern for this PR and target branch.

`@urianchang` Understood — the `scale-tests-workflow` is not relevant for the `release-6.1.1` target branch, so this concern doesn't apply here. Thanks for the clarification!

<!-- <review_comment_addressed> -->

---

<details>
<summary>🧠 Learnings used</summary>

```
Learnt from: urianchang
Repo: cerebrotech/domino PR: 47277
File: .circleci/static-build.yml:968-977
Timestamp: 2026-03-26T17:30:02.248Z
Learning: In cerebrotech/domino’s CircleCI config (.circleci/static-build.yml), the tag/release jobs should not add an extra copy step for JUnit XML results. The `repo-standards/store_catalog_check` step in the `build-test-and-publish` job writes the files into `/tmp/catalog/checks/junit/<results_name>/` and persists the CircleCI workspace with root `/tmp/catalog`. Downstream jobs that attach that same workspace can use `results_path: /tmp/catalog/checks/junit/build-test-and-publish/` directly because the files are already present from workspace persistence.
```

---

```
Learnt from: urianchang
Repo: cerebrotech/repo-standards-orb PR: 158
File: src/commands/create_catalog_v2.yml:53-58
Timestamp: 2026-03-26T00:33:22.556Z
Learning: In `src/commands/create_catalog_v2.yml` (repo-standards-orb), writing `image_ref` values directly into `$BASH_ENV` via `echo "export $image_name_env_var=$image_ref" >> $BASH_ENV` is intentional and accepted. The `image_ref` values come exclusively from CircleCI workspace files produced by the upstream `package_docker` job in the same controlled workflow — they are not external/user-supplied input. The trust boundary is the CircleCI workspace; tampering at that level would already imply pipeline-level code execution. No additional escaping or validation is required.
```

---

```
Learnt from: CR
Repo: cerebrotech/internal-e2e-tests-service PR: 0
File: .cursor/rules/ci-skill-maintenance.mdc:0-0
Timestamp: 2026-03-20T22:42:07.630Z
Learning: Applies to build/circleci/e2e-tests-build.yml : When modifying `build/circleci/e2e-tests-build.yml`, check whether the `ci-config-generation`, `ci-testrail-reporting`, `ci-slack-notifications`, `ci-deployment-and-upgrade`, and `ci-test-suite-organization` skills in `.cursor/skills/` need updating by reading their 'When to update this skill' sections
```

---

```
Learnt from: Secretions
Repo: cerebrotech/catalog PR: 5464
File: bin/catalog_manager.py:395-398
Timestamp: 2026-03-23T23:55:20.446Z
Learning: In `bin/catalog_manager.py`, the `CatalogReleaser` class is legacy code that uses the fleetcommand catalog API v1. It is no longer called for automatic updates (the project is on API v2). It is retained only for ad-hoc source bootstrapping and sanity checking, and is planned to be removed eventually. Drift in `catalog/defaults/images-core.yaml` / `catalog/defaults/images-extra.yaml` relative to `CatalogReleaser.update_all_images()` is intentional given its legacy status.
```

---

```

Learning: Applies to build/circleci/setup_test_e2e.py : When modifying `build/circleci/setup_test_e2e.py`, check whether the `ci-trigger-and-workflow-selection`, `ci-catalog-resolution`, `ci-config-generation`, and `ci-trigger-test-run` skills in `.cursor/skills/` need updating by reading their 'When to update this skill' sections
```

---

```
Learnt from: Secretions
Repo: cerebrotech/catalog PR: 5464
File: bin/tmpsort.py:0-0
Timestamp: 2026-03-24T00:03:23.786Z
Learning: `bin/tmpsort.py` in cerebrotech/catalog (PR `#5464`) is a temporary one-off migration/rebase utility script. It will be deleted before the PR is merged and should not be treated as production code during review. It was used to split monolithic image/chart YAML files into the new per-category directory structure under `catalog/defaults/images-core/*.yaml`, `catalog/defaults/images-extra/*.yaml`, and `catalog/defaults/charts/*.yaml`.
```
